title: Stenocereus beneckei
taxonomy: Stenocereus beneckei is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is a columnar cactus native to the Baja California Peninsula in Mexico. The classification of Stenocereus beneckei is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Caryophyllales, Family: Cactaceae, Genus: Stenocereus, Species: S. beneckei. This classification shows that Stenocereus beneckei is a flowering plant belonging to the cactus family. The species name "beneckei" is likely a tribute to a person, but the exact name is not publicly known.

intro: Stenocereus beneckei, also known as the Pitaya Blanca, is a species of cactus native to the deserts of northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, skin conditions, and infections.
